metadata.author: Core Team
metadata.description: Create a custom type (example: Box), then solve the stack transfer using your own model objects if you want.

introduces: separating data model from structure operation

messages.title: This level introduces custom types
messages.body: A custom type lets you model your own kind of value. The key lesson is that structure operations still move values the same way, even when those values represent richer objects you defined yourself.

messages.title: Do not confuse the type with the structure
messages.body: The type describes what a value is. The stack still decides how values move. If the transfer fails, separate the data model concept from the stack behavior concept.

messages.title: Start from the simpler layer
messages.body: If the custom type is distracting you, first solve the movement idea: how the value leaves A and reaches B. Then bring the custom model back into the solution.
